# 'gs ca --fill' uses the configured message updater
# to update the commit message.

as 'Test <test@example.com>'
at '2024-03-30T14:59:32Z'

# setup
cd repo
git init
git commit --allow-empty -m 'Initial commit'
gs repo init

git checkout -b feature
gs branch track --base main

# create initial commit
git add foo.txt
gs cc -m 'original message'

# configure the updater
chmod 755 $WORK/gen.sh
git config spice.message.generator $WORK/gen.sh

# amend using --fill
gs ca --fill

# verify the commit message was updated
git log -1 --format=%s
stdout 'updated message'

-- repo/foo.txt --
Contents of foo.
-- gen.sh --
#!/bin/sh
printf '{"title":"updated message"}'
